title: 6.06.2026 — Nika, Day 2
paragraphs: Two rather large ferries operate between Muhu and Saaremaa. We carefully crossed their path and entered the marina. We moored and went to explore the island. It is small. So charming and reminiscent of childhood. We made breakfast. Took showers. Refueled. After refueling, while filling the water tanks, I left a table knife on the pier—I had had to use it to pick at the hose nozzle. Around 17:00, we decided to cast off and head for the Gulf of Riga. The weather was hinting at trouble, but it didn't seem too daunting. Within the very first hour, we decided to prepare dinner. And that is when we noticed the knife was missing. Krzysztof had taken on the role of the ship's cook. He was quite upset that he would have to slice the provisions with his combat knife. Krzysztof is a Masurian Viking! He always has an axe and a blade on him. We cooked dinner, ate, and then it began...

Upon entering the gulf, visibility dropped to about 10 meters. The waves were choppy and aggressive. The wind grew squally. Sasha noticed the mast trembling. After a moment of hesitation, He gave the order to turn back. I remembered leaving the knife on the pier and suggested that perhaps it didn't want to be left alone without us, or maybe Nika refused to abandon it. We returned to Muhu once again and settled in for the night. We drank a can of beer each and went to sleep.
